toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_prospect_to_org_journey
PURPOSE: Combined view of prospects and organizations with promo code categorization and payment data
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table provides a unified view of the customer journey from prospect to organization,
including all records from both mysql_prospects and mysql_organizations tables.
It applies promo code categorization logic directly to prospects and includes
all columns from both tables (prospect columns prefixed with prospect_, org columns prefixed with org_).
Additionally includes first payment information from MRR data for conversion tracking.

GRANULARITY:
One row per combined prospect/organization record, including:
- Matched prospect-organization relationships (prospect and org columns populated). When a prospect has multiple organizations, the prospect data will be duplicated across multiple rows (one per organization).
- Prospects without any related organization (all org_* columns NULL).
- Organizations without any related prospect (all prospect_* columns NULL).

IMPORTANT - DUPLICATE WARNING:
This view can contain duplicate prospect_id values when a single prospect is associated 
with multiple organizations. Always use COUNT(DISTINCT prospect_id) when counting unique 
prospects to avoid inflated counts. The org_stripeid values are unique (no duplicates).

As of 2026-02-11, the distribution of prospect-to-organization relationships:
- 27,245 prospects have 1 organization (normal case)
- 55 prospects have 2 organizations
- 5 prospects have 3 organizations
- 1 prospect has 7 organizations (extreme edge case)

KEY COLUMNS:
- prospect_* columns: All columns from mysql_prospects table prefixed with prospect_ (Airbyte columns excluded)
- org_* columns: All columns from mysql_organizations table prefixed with org_ (Airbyte columns excluded)
- initial_positive_mrr_date: Date when customer first had positive MRR (NULL if never paid)
- promo_code_type: Categorized promo code type (YC, Stripe, Referral (Other Partners), Customer Referral, Marketing (No Promo Code), Marketing (Discount Code), No Related Prospect, or NULL)
  - NULL/empty prospects get 'Marketing (No Promo Code)' category
  - Specific marketing codes get 'Marketing (Discount Code)' category
  - Codes ending in 'stref' get 'Customer Referral' category
  - Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect' category
  - Uncategorized promo codes get NULL
- utm_type: Categorized UTM medium type (Marketing (Organic), Marketing (Paid), or No Related Prospect)
  - NULL/empty medium gets 'Marketing (Organic)' category
  - Paid channels (cpc, ppc, paidsocial, paid, newsletter, display, cpc\\, podcast) get 'Marketing (Paid)' category
  - Organic channels (organic, social, email, referral, post, description, card, getro.com) get 'Marketing (Organic)' category
  - Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect' category
- marketing_attribution: Final marketing attribution combining promo code and UTM data
  - YC, Stripe, Referral (Other Partners) keep their promo_code_type values
  - Marketing categories and Customer Referral use their utm_type values
  - Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect' category

DEPENDENCIES:
- public.mysql_prospects: Source table with prospect data
- public.mysql_organizations: Source table with organization data
- public.view_mrr_by_customer_by_day: MRR history for payment data

LAST MODIFIED: 2026-02-19
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_prospect_to_org_journey_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_prospect_to_org_journey_new
DISTKEY(prospect_id)
SORTKEY(prospect_createdat, org_createdat)
AS
WITH initial_payment_dates AS (
    /*
    Get the first day each customer had positive MRR from the view_mrr_by_customer_by_day table.
    
    WHY THIS APPROACH:
    - We need to identify when customers actually started paying (not just when they signed up)
    - Many customers start with trials or free tiers before converting to paid plans
    - Using MIN(event_day) WHERE mrr > 0 gives us the TRUE first payment date
    - This is more accurate than using day_number = 0 (which could be a trial start)
    - This is more accurate than active_start_mrr_change (which can occur multiple times per customer)
    
    DATA SOURCE:
    - view_mrr_by_customer_by_day: Our source of truth for MRR history
    - Contains one row per customer per day from their first Stripe event to latest
    - MRR values are forward-filled (last observation carried forward)
    
    ACCURACY NOTE:
    When compared to counting customers directly from view_mrr_by_customer_by_day,
    this join approach produces nearly identical results (99.3%+ match rate).
    Small discrepancies (<0.7% in any given month) may occur due to:
    - Customer IDs in MRR data without corresponding organizations
    - Data pipeline timing differences between tables
    - Edge cases where organizations exist without Stripe customers
    
    Example validation (2024-2026 data):
    - Direct from MRR view: 24,866 total new paying customers
    - Via this join: 24,852 total new paying customers  
    - Difference: 14 customers (0.056%)
    */
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        MIN(event_day) AS initial_positive_mrr_date
    FROM public.view_mrr_by_customer_by_day
    WHERE mrr > 0
    GROUP BY customer_id
),
base_data AS (
    SELECT 
    -- Prospect columns (prefixed with prospect_, excluding Airbyte columns)
    p.id AS prospect_id,
    p.plan AS prospect_plan,
    p.email AS prospect_email,
    p.gclid AS prospect_gclid,
    p.notes AS prospect_notes,
    p.state AS prospect_state,
    p.fbclid AS prospect_fbclid,
    p.medium AS prospect_medium,
    p.source AS prospect_source,
    p.keyword AS prospect_keyword,
    p.campaign AS prospect_campaign,
    p.interval AS prospect_interval,
    p.lastname AS prospect_lastname,
    p.referral AS prospect_referral,
    p.adgroupid AS prospect_adgroupid,
    p.createdat AS prospect_createdat,
    p.firstname AS prospect_firstname,
    p.partnerid AS prospect_partnerid,
    p.promocode AS prospect_promocode,
    p.updatedat AS prospect_updatedat,
    p.campaignid AS prospect_campaignid,
    p.gaclientid AS prospect_gaclientid,
    p.companyname AS prospect_companyname,
    p.initialstate AS prospect_initialstate,
    p.locationcode AS prospect_locationcode,
    p.referrerhost AS prospect_referrerhost,
    p.referrerpath AS prospect_referrerpath,
    p.stablesource AS prospect_stablesource,
    p.promocodename AS prospect_promocodename,
    p.qualification AS prospect_qualification,
    p.referrersearch AS prospect_referrersearch,
    p.landingpagehost AS prospect_landingpagehost,
    p.landingpagepath AS prospect_landingpagepath,
    p.registeredagents AS prospect_registeredagents,
    p.stripecustomerid AS prospect_stripecustomerid,
    p.clearbitpersonkey AS prospect_clearbitpersonkey,
    p.landingpagesearch AS prospect_landingpagesearch,
    p.clearbitcompanykey AS prospect_clearbitcompanykey,
    p.initiallocationcode AS prospect_initiallocationcode,
    p.failedpaymentmethodhold AS prospect_failedpaymentmethodhold,
    
    -- Organization columns (prefixed with org_, excluding Airbyte columns)
    o.id AS org_id,
    o.name AS org_name,
    o.hasbaa AS org_hasbaa,
    o.nickname AS org_nickname,
    o.stripeid AS org_stripeid,
    o.createdat AS org_createdat,
    o.updatedat AS org_updatedat,
    o.prospectid AS org_prospectid,
    o.outboundwebhookid AS org_outboundwebhookid,
    
    -- Promo code categorization logic
    CASE 
        -- Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect'
        WHEN p.id IS NULL AND o.id IS NOT NULL THEN 'No Related Prospect'
        -- Apply categorization for prospects
        WHEN p.id IS NOT NULL THEN
            CASE 
                -- YC codes get 'YC' category
                WHEN p.promocodename = 'yc'
                    OR p.promocodename = 'ycombinator'
                    OR p.promocodename ~ '^yc[fswx][0-9]{2}$'
                THEN 'YC'
                
                -- Stripe Atlas codes get 'Stripe' category
                WHEN p.promocodename ~ '^stripe(atlas|partner|atlaspartner)$'
                THEN 'Stripe'
                
                -- Other referral partner codes get 'Referral (Other Partners)' category
                WHEN p.promocodename IN (
                    'mercury',
                    'levy',
                    'chore',
                    'doola',
                    'central',
                    'pocketbook',
                    'blinkdigital',
                    'producthunt50',
                    'codi50',
                    'remofirst50',
                    'clerkydeal',
                    'openphone',
                    'arightco',
                    'rocketlawyerf50',
                    'mosey',
                    'speedinvest',
                    'vera',
                    'f6s',
                    'casium',
                    'gatewayx',
                    'northone',
                    'northoneplus',
                    'everyio',
                    'goprint',
                    'seamess',
                    'flex',
                    'justworks',
                    'cvconcepts',
                    'moxe',
                    'kmg',
                    'fptrial50',
                    'founderpass50grow',
                    'briars',
                    'thetaxvalet',
                    'pilot',
                    'cfoadvisors',
                    'ledgerly',
                    'cfoproject',
                    'burkland',
                    'buildingprofits',
                    'inventus',
                    'inventuslaw',
                    'wsgr',
                    'gunderson',
                    'cytlaw',
                    'epigram',
                    'ashbury',
                    'flowfi',
                    'fusion',
                    'deel',
                    'brex',
                    'pulley',
                    'clerky',
                    'hustlefund',
                    'sus',
                    '500startups',
                    'skydeck',
                    'ondeck',
                    'acceleprise',
                    'villageglobal',
                    'aircfo',
                    'magicbell',
                    'southparkcommons',
                    'levro',
                    'talentdrop',
                    'producthunt',
                    'tacostars',
                    'lombardstreet',
                    'fondo',
                    'stablefam',
                    'precursor20',
                    'precursor50'
                ) THEN 'Referral (Other Partners)'
                
                -- Customer Referral codes (ending in 'stref')
                WHEN p.promocodename ~ 'stref$' THEN 'Customer Referral'
                
                -- Marketing (No Promo Code) - NULL or empty promo codes
                WHEN COALESCE(p.promocodename, '') = '' THEN 'Marketing (No Promo Code)'
                
                -- Marketing (Discount Code) - specific marketing campaign codes
                WHEN p.promocodename IN (
                        '100creator2025',
                        '3monthsfree',
                        '50pros',
                        'abstractops',
                        'allthehacks',
                        'business50',
                        'cooley',
                        'customplan',
                        'efficient',
                        'emsoft',
                        'eoy25',
                        'fall50',
                        'florida',
                        'formation',
                        'gdsvfh',
                        'https://dashboard.usestable.com/onboard/begin?',
                        'katie50',
                        'kelly50',
                        'killb',
                        'novo',
                        'ph50',
                        'pod',
                        'redesignhealth',
                        'remote',
                        'scale20',
                        'scale50',
                        'stableil2024',
                        'stablema2024',
                        'stablewy2024',
                        'swap20',
                        'texas',
                        'tldr50',
                        'worklife'
                    ) THEN 'Marketing (Discount Code)'
                
                -- Everything else gets NULL (uncategorized/new codes)
                ELSE NULL
            END
        -- This shouldn't happen in a FULL OUTER JOIN, but keeping for completeness
        ELSE NULL
    END AS promo_code_type,
    
    -- UTM Type categorization based on medium field
    CASE 
        -- Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect'
        WHEN p.id IS NULL AND o.id IS NOT NULL THEN 'No Related Prospect'
        -- Apply categorization for prospects
        WHEN p.id IS NOT NULL THEN
            CASE 
                -- Paid channels get 'Marketing (Paid)' category
                WHEN p.medium IN (
                    'cpc',
                    'ppc', 
                    'paidsocial',
                    'paid',
                    'newsletter',
                    'display',
                    'cpc\\',
                    'podcast'
                ) THEN 'Marketing (Paid)'
                
                -- Everything else (including NULL/empty and organic channels) gets 'Marketing (Organic)'
                -- Explicitly listed organic channels: organic, social, email, referral, post, description, card, getro.com
                ELSE 'Marketing (Organic)'
            END
        -- This shouldn't happen in a FULL OUTER JOIN, but keeping for completeness
        ELSE NULL
    END AS utm_type

    FROM public.mysql_prospects p
    FULL OUTER JOIN public.mysql_organizations o
        ON p.id = o.prospectid
)
SELECT 
    base_data.*,
    -- Marketing Attribution - combines promo_code_type and utm_type logic
    CASE 
        WHEN base_data.promo_code_type = 'No Related Prospect' THEN 'No Related Prospect'
        WHEN base_data.promo_code_type IN ('YC', 'Stripe', 'Referral (Other Partners)') THEN base_data.promo_code_type
        -- For all other cases (Marketing categories and Customer Referral), use utm_type
        ELSE base_data.utm_type
    END AS marketing_attribution,
    /*
    Initial payment columns from MRR data
    These fields help track customer conversion from signup to payment:
    - initial_positive_mrr_date: When did the customer first have positive MRR? (NULL = never paid/still on trial)
    - initial_mrr: What was their initial payment amount?
    
    USAGE IN METABASE:
    To count new paying customers by month:
    1. Filter: initial_positive_mrr_date >= [your start date]
    2. Group by: initial_positive_mrr_date → Month
    3. Summarize: Count of Rows (or Count Distinct of org_stripeid)
    
    This gives you customers who ACTUALLY PAID (not just signed up) in each month.
    */
    initial_payment_dates.initial_positive_mrr_date,
    initial_payment_dates.initial_mrr
FROM base_data
-- Join on org_stripeid (Stripe customer ID) to match organizations with their payment history
-- LEFT JOIN ensures we keep all organizations, even those without payment data yet
LEFT JOIN initial_payment_dates ON initial_payment_dates.customer_id = base_data.org_stripeid;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old table and rename new table
BEGIN;
    DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_prospect_to_org_journey;
    ALTER TABLE view_prospect_to_org_journey_new RENAME TO view_prospect_to_org_journey;
COMMIT;

lines: ELSE base_data.utm_type
END AS marketing_attribution,

content.text:
Done! I've removed the `initial_mrr` column from:
1. The KEY COLUMNS documentation
2. The CTE SELECT statement
3. The final SELECT statement and its comments

Now the view only adds `initial_positive_mrr_date` for tracking when customers first paid.

message.content:
ok, now I have these comments!


    WHY THIS APPROACH:
    - We need to identify when customers actually started paying (not just when they signed up)
    - Many customers start with trials or free tiers before converting to paid plans
    - Using MIN(event_day) WHERE mrr > 0 gives us the TRUE first payment date

"TRUE first payment date" language is misleading, not true in all cases (for example, people who graduate from trials, have positive MRR, but haven't actually paid us yet -- and their payments might fail, they might never pay us)

- initial_positive_mrr_date: Date when customer first had positive MRR (NULL if never paid)
- initial_mrr: Initial MRR amount when customer first paid

the "when customer first paid" feels a little bit misleading: it's possible to have positive MRR before you have ever made a real $ payment

strictly speaking, the initial positive MRR date is exactly what the column name says: it's the first day with positive MRR

i think in general, let's clean up any language within the entire table definition that says anything about first payment, and clarify that it's just giving us the initial date that mrr was positive on please!

toolUseResult.originalFileContents:
/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_prospect_to_org_journey
PURPOSE: Combined view of prospects and organizations with promo code categorization and payment data
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table provides a unified view of the customer journey from prospect to organization,
including all records from both mysql_prospects and mysql_organizations tables.
It applies promo code categorization logic directly to prospects and includes
all columns from both tables (prospect columns prefixed with prospect_, org columns prefixed with org_).
Additionally includes first payment information from MRR data for conversion tracking.

GRANULARITY:
One row per combined prospect/organization record, including:
- Matched prospect-organization relationships (prospect and org columns populated). When a prospect has multiple organizations, the prospect data will be duplicated across multiple rows (one per organization).
- Prospects without any related organization (all org_* columns NULL).
- Organizations without any related prospect (all prospect_* columns NULL).

IMPORTANT - DUPLICATE WARNING:
This view can contain duplicate prospect_id values when a single prospect is associated 
with multiple organizations. Always use COUNT(DISTINCT prospect_id) when counting unique 
prospects to avoid inflated counts. The org_stripeid values are unique (no duplicates).

As of 2026-02-11, the distribution of prospect-to-organization relationships:
- 27,245 prospects have 1 organization (normal case)
- 55 prospects have 2 organizations
- 5 prospects have 3 organizations
- 1 prospect has 7 organizations (extreme edge case)

KEY COLUMNS:
- prospect_* columns: All columns from mysql_prospects table prefixed with prospect_ (Airbyte columns excluded)
- org_* columns: All columns from mysql_organizations table prefixed with org_ (Airbyte columns excluded)
- initial_positive_mrr_date: Date when customer first had positive MRR (NULL if never paid)
- promo_code_type: Categorized promo code type (YC, Stripe, Referral (Other Partners), Customer Referral, Marketing (No Promo Code), Marketing (Discount Code), No Related Prospect, or NULL)
  - NULL/empty prospects get 'Marketing (No Promo Code)' category
  - Specific marketing codes get 'Marketing (Discount Code)' category
  - Codes ending in 'stref' get 'Customer Referral' category
  - Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect' category
  - Uncategorized promo codes get NULL
- utm_type: Categorized UTM medium type (Marketing (Organic), Marketing (Paid), or No Related Prospect)
  - NULL/empty medium gets 'Marketing (Organic)' category
  - Paid channels (cpc, ppc, paidsocial, paid, newsletter, display, cpc\\, podcast) get 'Marketing (Paid)' category
  - Organic channels (organic, social, email, referral, post, description, card, getro.com) get 'Marketing (Organic)' category
  - Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect' category
- marketing_attribution: Final marketing attribution combining promo code and UTM data
  - YC, Stripe, Referral (Other Partners) keep their promo_code_type values
  - Marketing categories and Customer Referral use their utm_type values
  - Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect' category

DEPENDENCIES:
- public.mysql_prospects: Source table with prospect data
- public.mysql_organizations: Source table with organization data
- public.view_mrr_by_customer_by_day: MRR history for payment data

LAST MODIFIED: 2026-02-19
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_prospect_to_org_journey_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_prospect_to_org_journey_new
DISTKEY(prospect_id)
SORTKEY(prospect_createdat, org_createdat)
AS
WITH initial_payment_dates AS (
    /*
    Get the first day each customer had positive MRR from the view_mrr_by_customer_by_day table.
    
    WHY THIS APPROACH:
    - We need to identify when customers actually started paying (not just when they signed up)
    - Many customers start with trials or free tiers before converting to paid plans
    - Using MIN(event_day) WHERE mrr > 0 gives us the TRUE first payment date
    - This is more accurate than using day_number = 0 (which could be a trial start)
    - This is more accurate than active_start_mrr_change (which can occur multiple times per customer)
    
    DATA SOURCE:
    - view_mrr_by_customer_by_day: Our source of truth for MRR history
    - Contains one row per customer per day from their first Stripe event to latest
    - MRR values are forward-filled (last observation carried forward)
    
    ACCURACY NOTE:
    When compared to counting customers directly from view_mrr_by_customer_by_day,
    this join approach produces nearly identical results (99.3%+ match rate).
    Small discrepancies (<0.7% in any given month) may occur due to:
    - Customer IDs in MRR data without corresponding organizations
    - Data pipeline timing differences between tables
    - Edge cases where organizations exist without Stripe customers
    
    Example validation (2024-2026 data):
    - Direct from MRR view: 24,866 total new paying customers
    - Via this join: 24,852 total new paying customers  
    - Difference: 14 customers (0.056%)
    */
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        MIN(event_day) AS initial_positive_mrr_date
    FROM public.view_mrr_by_customer_by_day
    WHERE mrr > 0
    GROUP BY customer_id
),
base_data AS (
    SELECT 
    -- Prospect columns (prefixed with prospect_, excluding Airbyte columns)
    p.id AS prospect_id,
    p.plan AS prospect_plan,
    p.email AS prospect_email,
    p.gclid AS prospect_gclid,
    p.notes AS prospect_notes,
    p.state AS prospect_state,
    p.fbclid AS prospect_fbclid,
    p.medium AS prospect_medium,
    p.source AS prospect_source,
    p.keyword AS prospect_keyword,
    p.campaign AS prospect_campaign,
    p.interval AS prospect_interval,
    p.lastname AS prospect_lastname,
    p.referral AS prospect_referral,
    p.adgroupid AS prospect_adgroupid,
    p.createdat AS prospect_createdat,
    p.firstname AS prospect_firstname,
    p.partnerid AS prospect_partnerid,
    p.promocode AS prospect_promocode,
    p.updatedat AS prospect_updatedat,
    p.campaignid AS prospect_campaignid,
    p.gaclientid AS prospect_gaclientid,
    p.companyname AS prospect_companyname,
    p.initialstate AS prospect_initialstate,
    p.locationcode AS prospect_locationcode,
    p.referrerhost AS prospect_referrerhost,
    p.referrerpath AS prospect_referrerpath,
    p.stablesource AS prospect_stablesource,
    p.promocodename AS prospect_promocodename,
    p.qualification AS prospect_qualification,
    p.referrersearch AS prospect_referrersearch,
    p.landingpagehost AS prospect_landingpagehost,
    p.landingpagepath AS prospect_landingpagepath,
    p.registeredagents AS prospect_registeredagents,
    p.stripecustomerid AS prospect_stripecustomerid,
    p.clearbitpersonkey AS prospect_clearbitpersonkey,
    p.landingpagesearch AS prospect_landingpagesearch,
    p.clearbitcompanykey AS prospect_clearbitcompanykey,
    p.initiallocationcode AS prospect_initiallocationcode,
    p.failedpaymentmethodhold AS prospect_failedpaymentmethodhold,
    
    -- Organization columns (prefixed with org_, excluding Airbyte columns)
    o.id AS org_id,
    o.name AS org_name,
    o.hasbaa AS org_hasbaa,
    o.nickname AS org_nickname,
    o.stripeid AS org_stripeid,
    o.createdat AS org_createdat,
    o.updatedat AS org_updatedat,
    o.prospectid AS org_prospectid,
    o.outboundwebhookid AS org_outboundwebhookid,
    
    -- Promo code categorization logic
    CASE 
        -- Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect'
        WHEN p.id IS NULL AND o.id IS NOT NULL THEN 'No Related Prospect'
        -- Apply categorization for prospects
        WHEN p.id IS NOT NULL THEN
            CASE 
                -- YC codes get 'YC' category
                WHEN p.promocodename = 'yc'
                    OR p.promocodename = 'ycombinator'
                    OR p.promocodename ~ '^yc[fswx][0-9]{2}$'
                THEN 'YC'
                
                -- Stripe Atlas codes get 'Stripe' category
                WHEN p.promocodename ~ '^stripe(atlas|partner|atlaspartner)$'
                THEN 'Stripe'
                
                -- Other referral partner codes get 'Referral (Other Partners)' category
                WHEN p.promocodename IN (
                    'mercury',
                    'levy',
                    'chore',
                    'doola',
                    'central',
                    'pocketbook',
                    'blinkdigital',
                    'producthunt50',
                    'codi50',
                    'remofirst50',
                    'clerkydeal',
                    'openphone',
                    'arightco',
                    'rocketlawyerf50',
                    'mosey',
                    'speedinvest',
                    'vera',
                    'f6s',
                    'casium',
                    'gatewayx',
                    'northone',
                    'northoneplus',
                    'everyio',
                    'goprint',
                    'seamess',
                    'flex',
                    'justworks',
                    'cvconcepts',
                    'moxe',
                    'kmg',
                    'fptrial50',
                    'founderpass50grow',
                    'briars',
                    'thetaxvalet',
                    'pilot',
                    'cfoadvisors',
                    'ledgerly',
                    'cfoproject',
                    'burkland',
                    'buildingprofits',
                    'inventus',
                    'inventuslaw',
                    'wsgr',
                    'gunderson',
                    'cytlaw',
                    'epigram',
                    'ashbury',
                    'flowfi',
                    'fusion',
                    'deel',
                    'brex',
                    'pulley',
                    'clerky',
                    'hustlefund',
                    'sus',
                    '500startups',
                    'skydeck',
                    'ondeck',
                    'acceleprise',
                    'villageglobal',
                    'aircfo',
                    'magicbell',
                    'southparkcommons',
                    'levro',
                    'talentdrop',
                    'producthunt',
                    'tacostars',
                    'lombardstreet',
                    'fondo',
                    'stablefam',
                    'precursor20',
                    'precursor50'
                ) THEN 'Referral (Other Partners)'
                
                -- Customer Referral codes (ending in 'stref')
                WHEN p.promocodename ~ 'stref$' THEN 'Customer Referral'
                
                -- Marketing (No Promo Code) - NULL or empty promo codes
                WHEN COALESCE(p.promocodename, '') = '' THEN 'Marketing (No Promo Code)'
                
                -- Marketing (Discount Code) - specific marketing campaign codes
                WHEN p.promocodename IN (
                        '100creator2025',
                        '3monthsfree',
                        '50pros',
                        'abstractops',
                        'allthehacks',
                        'business50',
                        'cooley',
                        'customplan',
                        'efficient',
                        'emsoft',
                        'eoy25',
                        'fall50',
                        'florida',
                        'formation',
                        'gdsvfh',
                        'https://dashboard.usestable.com/onboard/begin?',
                        'katie50',
                        'kelly50',
                        'killb',
                        'novo',
                        'ph50',
                        'pod',
                        'redesignhealth',
                        'remote',
                        'scale20',
                        'scale50',
                        'stableil2024',
                        'stablema2024',
                        'stablewy2024',
                        'swap20',
                        'texas',
                        'tldr50',
                        'worklife'
                    ) THEN 'Marketing (Discount Code)'
                
                -- Everything else gets NULL (uncategorized/new codes)
                ELSE NULL
            END
        -- This shouldn't happen in a FULL OUTER JOIN, but keeping for completeness
        ELSE NULL
    END AS promo_code_type,
    
    -- UTM Type categorization based on medium field
    CASE 
        -- Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect'
        WHEN p.id IS NULL AND o.id IS NOT NULL THEN 'No Related Prospect'
        -- Apply categorization for prospects
        WHEN p.id IS NOT NULL THEN
            CASE 
                -- Paid channels get 'Marketing (Paid)' category
                WHEN p.medium IN (
                    'cpc',
                    'ppc', 
                    'paidsocial',
                    'paid',
                    'newsletter',
                    'display',
                    'cpc\\',
                    'podcast'
                ) THEN 'Marketing (Paid)'
                
                -- Everything else (including NULL/empty and organic channels) gets 'Marketing (Organic)'
                -- Explicitly listed organic channels: organic, social, email, referral, post, description, card, getro.com
                ELSE 'Marketing (Organic)'
            END
        -- This shouldn't happen in a FULL OUTER JOIN, but keeping for completeness
        ELSE NULL
    END AS utm_type

    FROM public.mysql_prospects p
    FULL OUTER JOIN public.mysql_organizations o
        ON p.id = o.prospectid
)
SELECT 
    base_data.*,
    -- Marketing Attribution - combines promo_code_type and utm_type logic
    CASE 
        WHEN base_data.promo_code_type = 'No Related Prospect' THEN 'No Related Prospect'
        WHEN base_data.promo_code_type IN ('YC', 'Stripe', 'Referral (Other Partners)') THEN base_data.promo_code_type
        -- For all other cases (Marketing categories and Customer Referral), use utm_type
        ELSE base_data.utm_type
    END AS marketing_attribution,
    /*
    Initial payment date from MRR data
    This field helps track customer conversion from signup to payment:
    - initial_positive_mrr_date: When did the customer first have positive MRR? (NULL = never paid/still on trial)
    
    USAGE IN METABASE:
    To count new paying customers by month:
    1. Filter: initial_positive_mrr_date >= [your start date]
    2. Group by: initial_positive_mrr_date → Month
    3. Summarize: Count of Rows (or Count Distinct of org_stripeid)
    
    This gives you customers who ACTUALLY PAID (not just signed up) in each month.
    */
    initial_payment_dates.initial_positive_mrr_date
FROM base_data
-- Join on org_stripeid (Stripe customer ID) to match organizations with their payment history
-- LEFT JOIN ensures we keep all organizations, even those without payment data yet
LEFT JOIN initial_payment_dates ON initial_payment_dates.customer_id = base_data.org_stripeid;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old table and rename new table
BEGIN;
    DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_prospect_to_org_journey;
    ALTER TABLE view_prospect_to_org_journey_new RENAME TO view_prospect_to_org_journey;
COMMIT;

-PURPOSE: Combined view of prospects and organizations with promo code categorization and payment data
+PURPOSE: Combined view of prospects and organizations with promo code categorization and MRR data
DESCRIPTION:
including all records from both mysql_prospects and mysql_organizations tables.
It applies promo code categorization logic directly to prospects and includes
all columns from both tables (prospect columns prefixed with prospect_, org columns prefixed with org_).
-Additionally includes first payment information from MRR data for conversion tracking.
+Additionally includes the initial positive MRR date from MRR data for conversion tracking.

One row per combined prospect/organization record, including:

- org_* columns: All columns from mysql_organizations table prefixed with org_ (Airbyte columns excluded)

- Specific marketing codes get 'Marketing (Discount Code)' category

content.text:
Done! I've cleaned up all the misleading language throughout the file:

1. **Changed "payment data" → "MRR data"** in the header and dependencies
2. **Changed "first payment" → "first positive MRR"** everywhere
3. **Removed "TRUE first payment date"** language - now just says "first date with positive MRR"
4. **Added clarification note**: "Positive MRR does not necessarily mean a payment has been collected. Customers can have positive MRR while on trial or before their first payment is processed."
5. **Updated CTE name** from `initial_payment_dates` to `initial_positive_mrr_dates`
6. **Fixed NULL descriptions** from "never paid" to "never had positive MRR"
7. **Updated validation language** from "new paying customers" to "customers with positive MRR"

The documentation now accurately reflects that this column tracks when MRR became positive, not when actual payments were collected.
